rationale: Both correctly land on the actual resolution (invalid — core_model run-level silently injects dummy weights; fix is --run-level=full_model), matching the thread's close. X cites the same _add_dummy_load_format mechanism but leaves an unresolved paradox (it says the test is marked full_model yet doesn't explain why that marker didn't prevent running under core_model) and adds a tangential A100/GPU-memory-tuning aside not supported by the thread, which could mislead the reporter. Y explicitly resolves that marker paradox (selection marker vs. run-level flag are independent), grounds the mechanism across more of the actual test harness (run_args.py, runtime.py, stage_config.py, assertions.py), and adds concrete practical requirements (video path existence, real weights, memory split) plus a reasonable non-blocking follow-up suggestion — making it more complete and slightly better grounded without contradicting the ground truth.
